description: Create a 5-species dichotomous key for Pacific Northwest conifers, set up their taxonomic species profiles, and record 3 structured field observations in TiddlyWiki. Create five Species Profile tiddlers tagged 'Species' with custom fields for scientific-name, family, and conservation-status: 'Western Redcedar' (Thuja plicata, Cupressaceae, Least Concern), 'Ponderosa Pine' (Pinus ponderosa, Pinaceae, Least Concern), 'Sitka Spruce' (Picea sitchensis, Pinaceae, Least Concern), 'Douglas-fir' (Pseudotsuga menziesii, Pinaceae, Least Concern), and 'Western Hemlock' (Tsuga heterophylla, Pinaceae, Least Concern). Construct a dichotomous key using four tiddlers tagged 'KeyNode' containing choice text and links in the body: 'Key: Start' (Choice A: Leaves are scale-like, links to 'Western Redcedar'; Choice B: Leaves are needle-like, links to 'Key: Needles'), 'Key: Needles' (Choice A: Needles arranged in bundles, links to 'Ponderosa Pine'; Choice B: Needles attached singly, links to 'Key: Single Needles'), 'Key: Single Needles' (Choice A: Needles are sharp, stiff, and 4-sided, links to 'Sitka Spruce'; Choice B: Needles are flat and flexible, links to 'Key: Flat Needles'), and 'Key: Flat Needles' (Choice A: Cones have distinct 3-pronged bracts, links to 'Douglas-fir'; Choice B: Cones are very small, links to 'Western Hemlock'). Record three field observation tiddlers tagged 'Observation' with custom fields for date, latitude, longitude, and identified-species: 'Obs-001' (20240512, 47.7511, -123.4312, Douglas-fir), 'Obs-002' (20240513, 47.7922, -123.4111, Western Redcedar), and 'Obs-003' (20240514, 47.8105, -123.3551, Sitka Spruce).
